generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T ESERINUM. (Physostigmin.) An alkaloid of the Calabar bean. C 30 H 20 N 3 O 4 . Authorities. Dr. A. Von Reuss, Am. Hom. Obs., vol. xv, 1878, p. 195, effects on the eye. Leopold Grossegger, æt. sixteen years. In left eye a traumatic cataract. with right eye reads No. 40 Sn. at 15 feet, with convex 16 the same, with concave cyl. axis vert., reads No. 30, the measurement before application of drug gave r = 7.5150. Three drops from a 1 per cent. sol. were applied, and the following results obtained: Hm 1/24 S 15/40 (after five minutes); m 1/16 S 15/40 (after ten minutes); r = 7.5205; m 1/20 (after fifteen minutes); r = 7.4435 (after twenty minutes); m 1/16 (after twenty-five minutes); r = 5.44625 (after thirty minutes); m 1/10 (after thirty-five minutes); r = 7.4270 (after forty minutes); m 1/9 (after forty-five minutes); r = 7.4265 (after fifty minutes); m 1/12, immediately thereafter m 1/14 (after fifty-five minutes); r = 7.4435 (after sixty minutes); m 1/20 S 13/80, directly afterwards m 1/80 S 15/30 (after sixty-five minutes); r = 7.4105 (after seventy minutes); emmetropia (after seventy-five minutes); r = 7.42425 (after eighty minutes); Hm 1/30 (after eighty-five minutes); r = 7.47375 (after ninety minutes); Hm 1/30 S 15/40, same with + 18 cyl. axis vert. (after ninety-five minutes); r = 7.515 (after one hundred minutes); Hm 1/24 S 15/40, aslo with + 18 c. ax. vert. (after one hundred and five minutes); r = 7.5277 (after one hundred and ten minutes); Hm 1/24 S 15/4 0, also with + 18 c. ax. vert. (one hundred and fifteen minutes); r = 7.5205 (after one hundred and twenty minutes). Pupil at close of measurement still very small. Extreme tension in bulb, and for some time sensations of pain. No headache. The increase of radius during first five minutes is due to a failure in measurement. From this point it diminished rapidly and reached its maximum in seventy minutes. Entire difference 0.1 mm. The increase was slower, and required one hundred and ten minutes to return to the normal. The greatest increase of the refraction up to m 1/9 amount to 1/5.8 refracting value, and occurred at the 40th minute. At the close of the experiment, after two hours, the refraction was still normal. Joseph Stichanel, æt. forty years. Left eye. Emmetropic S 20/20. Two measurements before use of sol., gave r = 7.625. When one application of 1 per cent. sol. was made, and measurements taken, at first every five minutes, later every ten, in intervals vision tests were applied. After first five minutes the pupil was already smaller, and after twenty was very small. R = 7.61675 m 1/20 (after five minutes); r = 7.61675 m 1/14 (after ten minutes); r = 7.62675 m 1/10 (after fifteen minutes); r = 7.62500 m 1/9 (after twenty minutes); r = 7.61675 m 1/12 (after twenty-five minutes); r = 7.62675 m 1/14 (after thirty minutes); r = 7.60575 m 1/14 (after thirty-five minutes); r = 7.62675 m 1/14 (after forty minutes); r = 7.53425 m 1/14 (after forty-five minutes); r = 7.57825 m 1/18 (after fifty-five minutes); r = 7.60025 m 1/20 (after sixty-five minutes); r = 7.62675 m 1/20 (after seventy-five minutes); r = 7.62500 m 1/40 (after eighty-five minutes); m 1/60 (after ninety minutes). The action here is not so satisfactory as in the previous case, a marked sinking of radius being first noticed after the 40th minute, but suddenly. Up to this time the changes are minimum. The maximum of diminution is 0.09 mm., and remains at this only a few minutes. The increase took place more slowly, and after seventy-five minutes the primary value is again reached. The increase of the refraction began immediately after the application, increased gradually until at the 20th minute the highest point was reached with m 1/9 (refracting power 1/9), then declined very slowly, so that after ninety minutes there exists a myopia of 1/60. Experiments three and four not being very satisfactory in results, I will not introduce them. Will, however, say that in number three the twitching and pain of a tearing character were quite marked during early part of experiment. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
Clinical: Blepharospasm; Ciliary spasm; Glaucoma; Headache; Pupil, contracted; Salivation; Sweat, excessive
The child lay apathetic on the bed, pale and collapsed.

keynotes.text: Eserine is best known as used by oculists to procure contraction of the pupils in glaucoma. It has been used by Fenoch of Berlin (H. R., iv. 127) in a case of chorea in a child of nine. Half a millegramme of Eser. sulph. was injected into the right upper arm, and the following proving was the result: In fifteen minutes the child cried out loudly, vomited profusely, and complained of violent headache. Soon the face and upper part of the body became covered with profuse sweat. Half an hour later the flow of saliva was increased. The heart-beats sank to 64, radial artery contracted, pulse small and thready. After repeated vomitings signs of cardiac weakness increased in spite of remedies, whilst sweat and saliva decreased, and in one half-hour had disappeared. A moderate contraction of the pupil took place, reaction slow. The child lay apathetic on the bed, pale and collapsed. Antidotes were administered, and the child was well next morning. A remark of Mittendorf that "spasm of the accommodation is also produced artificially by Eserin." led R. B. Leach (Med. Adv., xix. 262) to prescribe successfully the 6x trituration in cases of ciliary spasm, and of astigmatism due to irregular action of the ciliary muscles. Eserin. has also produced blepharospasm. A solution of one grain to the drachm (mixed in mistake for a grain to the ounce) was instilled into the eye of a lady of 60 suffering from cataract. Sweating and lachrymation followed, and, as these passed off, in a quarter of an hour "clonic spasm of the eyelids" set in, "the upper lid failing down on the lower every few seconds." Spasmodic feeling in lips and the same sensation under jaw of left side followed. Feeling of tremor and spasm, without retinal movement, followed in an hour. Pupils contracted to small point.

eyes.text: Moderate contraction of pupils; reaction slow, by shading the eyes they did not enlarge; on exposure to strong light they contracted slightly, but returned to their normal size. Spasm of accommodation; ciliary spasm. Pain and irritation of eyes on continued reading. Blepharospasm.

stomach_abdomen.text: Vomited profusely and complained of headache. Repeated vomiting with signs of cardiac weakness.

chest_heart.text: After repeated vomiting signs of cardiac weakness increased. Heart-beats sank to 64; radial artery contracted; pulse small and thready.

fever_chill.text: Face and upper part of the body covered with profuse perspiration. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.
